page: Microsoft Azure EC32ads_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECADSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 6.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 1229 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options for external disk attachments. It does not include any integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the EC32ads_v5 offers a qualitative balance between memory capacity and compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that also require local scratch space. Tradeoffs include the lack of specialized hardware accelerators like GPUs and the absence of bundled public IPv4 addresses. Common workloads for this configuration include relational databases, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC32ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ECADSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, 192.0 GB of system memory, and 1229 GB of local storage. With a memory-to-core ratio of 6.0 GB per vCPU, this server provides a high-density memory configuration alongside local storage capabilities. It supports Premium Storage but does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, enterprise applications, and database deployments requiring substantial local disk space.
